(auto) ensure agent ends day at home

diary: Woke up to that kind of grey that makes the whole house feel quieter, like the rain is thinking. By 5:45 I had coffee going and did my usual ceramicist checklist—kiln days on the brain, hands already itching to keep everything consistent. Bagel Delite in Issaquah felt bright in comparison, even with the drizzle hanging in the air. I aimed for the Bothell area early, mostly to dodge the SR-520 eastbound mess that started up right around 9. The studio at Bothell Ceramics Studio was all soft clinks and patient glaze work: small bowls, mug handles, thickness checks like I was measuring the mood of the day. Crown Hill Cafe gave me a quick warm reset. REI after that for packaging and a few touch-up bits—practical comfort. Evening found me back at Issaquah, changing out of wet clothes and following my mushroom notes to Tiger Mountain. It’s funny how damp weather can still feel like an invitation. I fell asleep tired, but good and calm.
thought_process: I planned my route and my work rhythm around the rain and the SR-520 closure without making it a big dramatic thing. Leaving early toward Bothell helped me avoid the cross-lake snarls, and once I was at the studio, I leaned into tasks that tolerate slow, steady conditions—glazing consistency, finishing, drying checks, and packing pieces. I kept errands nearby (REI felt like the right kind of detour) so I wouldn’t burn time fighting weather or traffic. The drizzle made me slower in a good way; I paid attention more, like the day was asking for detail. After the warm dinner, my mood shifted toward restorative focus, and the damp trail at Tiger Mountain felt like a reward for sticking to the plan.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
